questions.text: What is the main theme of Surah Ad-Dukhan regarding warning and mercy?
questions.answer: It warns of punishment for denial while reminding that Allah sends revelation as mercy and guidance.

questions.text: Why does the Surah emphasize a 'blessed night' connected to revelation?
questions.answer: To show the honor of revelation and that Allah decrees wise matters with perfect knowledge.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah's control over major decisions in creation?
questions.answer: Allah decrees matters with wisdom, and nothing happens outside His knowledge and command.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests the universe exists without truth or purpose.
questions.answer: False. It states Allah created the heavens and earth in truth, though many people do not realize.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about people who only 'believe' when punishment becomes visible?
questions.answer: They may promise belief under pressure, but many return to denial, showing insincerity.

questions.text: What is the lesson from asking for punishment to be removed and then returning to disbelief?
questions.answer: That temporary fear is not real faith; true faith requires sincerity and lasting change.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Musa (AS) confronting Pharaoh?
questions.answer: That Musa came with a trustworthy message calling to free the believers and submit to Allah.

questions.text: How does the Surah portray Pharaoh's people as a warning for tyrants?
questions.answer: Their worldly power did not save them; Allah drowned them and removed their legacy.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention the heavens and earth not weeping for Pharaoh's people?
questions.answer: To show how insignificant and rejected they became due to their ظلم and denial.

questions.text: What is one reflection-based lesson about legacy from this Surah?
questions.answer: Worldly possessions and buildings can be left behind instantly; only faith and deeds endure.

questions.text: What does Surah Ad-Dukhan teach about signs like the 'smoke'?
questions.answer: They are warnings meant to awaken people before final accountability, not entertainment or rumors.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah's warnings come without any prior mercy.
questions.answer: False. It shows warnings are sent so people can repent before judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Sorting' (Yawm al-Fasl) in this Surah's message?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment when truth is separated from falsehood and every person is recompensed.

questions.text: Why is the Day of Sorting described as appointed for everyone?
questions.answer: To emphasize certainty: no one escapes it, and no one can delay it once it arrives.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the humiliation of sinners in Hell?
questions.answer: They are fed bitter punishment and addressed sarcastically for the pride they had in the world.

questions.text: What is the spiritual lesson from the sarcastic phrase 'mighty, noble' said to a sinner?
questions.answer: Worldly arrogance is exposed as false; true honor comes from obedience to Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Tree of Zaqqum?
questions.answer: It is a painful food for sinners, showing punishment is real and fitting for rebellion.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah portrays Hell as symbolic and not real.
questions.answer: False. It describes Hell vividly as real punishment.

questions.text: How does Surah Ad-Dukhan motivate hope for the righteous?
questions.answer: It describes them in secure places with gardens, springs, and honor.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about safety and security for the muttaqin?
questions.answer: They are in a secure place and protected from fear and punishment.

questions.text: What does it mean that the people of Paradise will not taste death except the first death?
questions.answer: They will live eternally; death will not return after entering Paradise.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect 'great success' to the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Great success is being saved from Hell and granted Allah's protection and Paradise.

questions.text: What does this Surah teach about the danger of being deceived by worldly life?
questions.answer: People can ignore warnings and mock truth until sudden punishment exposes their loss.

questions.text: Why does the Surah include examples like Tubba and earlier peoples?
questions.answer: To show patterns of denial and to warn that power without righteousness ends in destruction.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah implies that being a 'strong civilization' guarantees Allah's protection.
questions.answer: False. Strength without faith and justice does not protect from Allah's punishment.

questions.text: How does Surah Ad-Dukhan teach patience to the Prophet and believers?
questions.answer: It commands waiting and watching, trusting Allah's timing and the certainty of the outcome.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Quran being 'made easy'?
questions.answer: That Allah made it accessible so people can remember and take guidance seriously.

questions.text: What is one practical way to benefit from the Quran being made easy?
questions.answer: Recite it with reflection, learn its meanings, and apply its teachings in daily life.

questions.text: How does the Surah frame revelation as mercy?
questions.answer: Revelation is sent to warn and guide before punishment, giving people a chance to repent.

questions.text: What is one key lesson about sincerity in faith from this Surah?
questions.answer: Real faith remains after hardship passes; it is not only words spoken in fear.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach the seriousness of lying and denying the message?
questions.answer: It links denial to punishment and shows that warnings were clear and repeated.

questions.text: What does the Surah imply about Allah's justice in historical punishment?
questions.answer: Punishments are not random; they are tied to persistent injustice, arrogance, and rejection of truth.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that people can rely on second chances after death.
questions.answer: False. It highlights an appointed time and irreversible consequences after judgment.

questions.text: What is one daily-life lesson from the Surah about pride and status?
questions.answer: Do not let status make you arrogant; Allah can remove everything instantly.

questions.text: How can the Surah help someone who fears their sins and consequences?
questions.answer: By pushing them toward repentance now and reminding them of the reality of judgment and mercy.

questions.text: What does Surah Ad-Dukhan teach about the link between knowledge and accountability?
questions.answer: Since the message is clear and easy, rejection becomes a deliberate choice with consequences.

questions.text: Why is the contrast between Hell's food and Paradise's comfort important?
questions.answer: It highlights the moral seriousness of choices and motivates people to seek Allah's pleasure.

questions.text: What is the Surah's warning about delaying repentance?
questions.answer: Punishment and the Hour can come suddenly, and regret afterward may not benefit.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway from Surah Ad-Dukhan?
questions.answer: Take revelation seriously, repent sincerely, and prepare for the Day of Sorting.

questions.text: What is the Surah's strongest call to action for a modern reader?
questions.answer: Reflect on Allah's warnings, abandon arrogance and denial, and commit to faith and righteousness before it is too late.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that Allah's control extends over history and nations?
questions.answer: By showing how entire civilizations rose and fell by Allah's decree due to their choices.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the honor of the righteous beyond worldly honor?
questions.answer: Their honor is security and eternal life, not temporary power.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah implies the Prophet should compromise the message to reduce conflict.
questions.answer: False. It tells him to remain firm and wait, trusting Allah's outcome.

questions.text: What does Surah Ad-Dukhan teach about Allah's mercy in giving reminders repeatedly?
questions.answer: Repeated reminders are opportunities to return; ignoring them increases blame.

questions.text: How can this Surah affect a believer's relationship with luxury and comfort?
questions.answer: It pushes them to value the Hereafter over comfort and to use blessings with gratitude and obedience.

questions.text: What is one misconception about punishment that this Surah corrects?
questions.answer: That punishment is distant or unlikely; the Surah shows it can be near and sudden.

questions.text: How does the Surah strengthen certainty (yaqin) about the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Through vivid descriptions of judgment, Hell, Paradise, and historical examples.

questions.text: What is one ethical lesson from Musa's message 'deliver to me the servants of Allah'?
questions.answer: Oppression of believers and denial of truth are major wrongs that Allah does not ignore.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about real safety compared to worldly safety?
questions.answer: Real safety is being protected from Allah's punishment, not merely being comfortable in the world.

questions.text: What is the Surah's overall spiritual goal?
questions.answer: To awaken hearts to revelation, warn against denial, and guide toward repentance and salvation.

questions.text: What does 'Ad-Dukhan' mean?
questions.answer: The Smoke.

questions.text: On which blessed night does the Surah mention the Quran being sent down?
questions.answer: On a blessed night (Laylat al-Qadr).

questions.text: What happens in the 'Blessed Night' regarding the affairs of the world?
questions.answer: Every matter of wisdom is made distinct.

questions.text: What is the 'visible smoke' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: A sign that will envelop the people as a painful punishment.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers react after seeing signs?
questions.answer: They would say, 'Our Lord, remove from us the punishment; indeed, we are believers,' but then they would return to disbelief.

questions.text: What was the 'Message' Musa (AS) brought to the people of Pharaoh?
questions.answer: 'Render to me the servants of Allah (Bani Israel). Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger'.

questions.text: What happened to Pharaoh's army in the sea according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They were drowned after pursuing Musa (AS) and the believers.

questions.text: Did the heavens and earth weep for the destroyed people of Pharaoh?
questions.answer: No. The Surah states the heaven and earth did not weep for them, and they were not given respite.

questions.text: Who are the 'People of Tubba' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: A powerful ancient civilization in Yemen that was destroyed for their sins.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Sorting' (Yawm al-Fasl)?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment, the appointed time for everyone.

questions.text: What will be the food of the sinners in Hell?
questions.answer: The tree of Zaqqum.

questions.text: How is the effect of Zaqqum described in the belly?
questions.answer: Like murky oil, it boils in the bellies like the boiling of scalding water.

questions.text: What will be said to the sinner while being punished in Hell?
questions.answer: 'Taste! Indeed, you are the mighty, the noble!' (sarcastic reference to their worldly pride).

questions.text: Where will the righteous (Muttaqin) be on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: In a secure place, among gardens and springs.

questions.text: What will the dwellers of Paradise wear?
questions.answer: Garments of fine silk and brocade.

questions.text: Will the people in Paradise taste death again?
questions.answer: No, they will not taste death therein except for the first death (in the world).

questions.text: What is the 'Great Success' mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Being protected by Allah from the punishment of Hellfire.

questions.text: Why did Allah make the Quran easy in the Prophet's language according to this Surah?
questions.answer: So that people might be reminded and take heed.

questions.text: What should the Prophet do at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Wait and watch, for the disbelievers are also waiting.

questions.text: Was the universe created for 'play'?
questions.answer: No, Allah created the heavens and earth in truth, but most people do not know.
